significance: Zurich: every line passes through HB creating overlap. City101: one main line Geneva-Lausanne-Villeneuve with minimal branching.
City101 corridor is 3x the length — but with less than half the frequency variation.
City101 has more stations but many are barely served.
Hub comparison (ZH HB vs Lausanne). Roughly equivalent — both hubs are strong.
THE critical number. Zurich's worst station (Oberrieden 2/hr) vs City101's worst (St-Saphorin 0.0/hr). The floor never drops to zero in Zurich.
Zurich: 24/2 = 12x variation. City101: 28.5/2.0 = ~14x if excluding zero or 42x including all. Radial networks compress variation.
City101 average is actually higher because Geneva/Lausanne inflate it. But the distribution is bimodal — most stations cluster near the floor.
Only 2 of 12 Zurich stations below 3/hr (both at exactly 2). 6 of 49 City101 stations below 3/hr including two at zero.
Zurich median is lower — but more evenly distributed. City101 median hides extreme tails.
St-Saphorin has NO scheduled trains during workday morning. Zero Zurich stations have this condition.
Both networks have single-line minimum stations. But Zurich's single-line stations still get 2/hr; City101's get 2-3/hr at best.
HB has 16 S-Bahn lines — incomparable to City101 which doesn't have a radial overlay system.
But Zurich's IR is redundant (S-Bahn covers the same stations). City101's IR is the ONLY express service.
All 12 ZH stations get ≥2/hr. 47/49 City101 stations get ≥2/hr. But City101's 2/hr stations are 60km from any hub.
10/12 ZH stations get ≥4/hr. ~22/49 City101 stations get ≥4/hr. This is where the radial advantage shows.
